mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and yields positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of co-occurring instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signals capacity constraints and supply-side bottlenecks, triggering inflation expectations and monetary tightening that suppress S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 60-day returns in over 60 percent of cases, or the regime-specific coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer expenditure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or null correlation with 20-day forward returns across a 5-year walk-forward test, with Rank IC consistently below 0.015.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230.0, elevated inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive central bank policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in 70 percent of weeks where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for concurrent rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates underlying demand resilience; when both series trend upward together, it confirms sustainable consumption that drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS trend signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over 3-year rolling windows, or fails to outperform a single-series baseline in out-of-sample forecasts.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in industrial production and payroll employment during a post-shock recovery phase signals robust capacity utilization and labor absorption, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns in 65 percent of post-shock recovery months, or the regime-specific predictive accuracy drops below 52 percent in walk-forward testing.
